steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The left side has the noun phrase “the researchers” (subject); the right side has “computer imaging to compare the lengths of eggs from species like Chloroceryle americana and Francolinus coqui“ followed by the main verb “aimed.” The blank needs a verb form that bridges the subject and the following phrase.

steps.title: Apply the participle rule
steps.content: A present participle (-ing form) creates a participial phrase that modifies the subject. “Using” correctly forms “using computer imaging to compare the lengths of eggs from species like Chloroceryle americana and Francolinus coqui“ to describe how the researchers aimed to shed light on the variation. The other forms don't work: “to use” (B) is an infinitive, “use” (C) is a bare verb, and “used” (D) creates structural confusion. That matches choice A — A is correct.
